methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Andrenam is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Matej Cernosek and Alex Chu that has raised $10M to date. The broader sector comp universe includes 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, with notable private comparables including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. Using total capital raised ($10M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next funding stage yields $25M. A peer-median approach cannot be applied; while 19 same-sector peers exist, the narrow filters for stage and valuation tier returned insufficient datapoints (0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier peers), falling short of the three-comparable threshold needed for reliable cross-sectional benchmarking.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median point of $25M, derived from a single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the strength of Andrenam's technical differentiation relative to the broader Defense & Security peer set, the company's revenue and growth trajectory (not provided), and the applicability of sector-wide multiples to an early-stage company. Given the single-method foundation, expansion of comparable data—particularly same-stage or adjacent-stage Defense peers—would strengthen conviction in this range.
